key_themes: Ongoing £20m share buyback program with multiple tranches executed and shares cancelled
Strong FY2025 results with 22% adjusted PBT growth and margin target exceeded
Upgraded FY26 guidance expected to beat consensus estimates
Operational improvements and acquisitions driving recovery and growth
Analyst Buy consensus with significant price target upside (~50% from current levels)
summary: Restore plc (AIM: RST.L), a UK provider of data, information, and asset management services, is generating positive sentiment after robust FY2025 results that showed 22% adjusted profit growth, margin expansion above target, and strong second-half momentum. The company launched a £20m share buyback programme (with ongoing purchases and cancellations announced throughout March and April 2026) and guided for FY26 adjusted PBT slightly ahead of consensus, prompting positive share price reactions and analyst support. Social media and retail investor discussion on X remains quiet, with sentiment primarily driven by official news flow rather than viral conversation.[[1]](https://www.ajbell.co.uk/news/articles/restore-expects-beat-profit-consensus-2026-after-positive-2025)[[2]](https://www.londonstockexchange.com/news-article/RST/full-year-2025-results/17499108)[[3]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/LON/RST/)
sources: 12+ news/RNS articles (FY2025 results, repeated buyback announcements in Mar/Apr 2026), Very limited relevant X posts (low engagement, <5 high-quality matches; mostly corporate updates or unrelated)
